template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — PERSONAL INJURY CONTINGENCY FEE AGREEMENT**:
Formal contract signed by the client and the Firm. Governed by ABA Model Rule 1.5(c), which requires a contingent fee agreement to be IN WRITING, signed by the client, and to state (i) the method by which the fee is to be determined, including the percentage or percentages, (ii) the expenses to be deducted before or after the fee is calculated, and (iii) whether such expenses are to be paid by the client regardless of outcome. Target length: 3–5 pages. Use numbered sections (`1.`, `2.`, …) with bold section titles, followed by full-prose paragraphs. Use "Client" and "Firm" throughout (avoid first-person).

DO NOT emit a document-title heading other than the required `CONTINGENCY FEE AGREEMENT` banner at the very top (centered, bold, all caps). This banner is required regardless of stationery — it identifies the instrument as a contract.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `feePercentage`: numeric or object. When numeric (e.g., `33.33`), a flat contingent percentage applies at all stages. When an object `{preSuit, postSuit, postTrial}` (e.g., `{"preSuit": 33.33, "postSuit": 40, "postTrial": 45}`), emit tiered language. Default: `{"preSuit": 33.33, "postSuit": 40}`.
- `costsHandling`: `advanced_by_firm` (default) | `client_pays_upfront` | `hybrid` — controls Section 5 (Costs and Expenses).
- `feeBase`: `net` (default — fee computed AFTER deducting costs) | `gross` (fee computed BEFORE deducting costs). Note: state ethics rules vary on what is permissible; this generic template uses `net` as the default because it is the more client-protective option. Firms should confirm state-specific rules.
- `includeArbitration`: boolean (default false) — when true, Section 10 adds a mandatory-arbitration clause for fee or malpractice disputes (with express carve-out notice). Firms should use with care; many state ethics opinions restrict mandatory arbitration of malpractice claims.
- `includeLien`: boolean (default true) — when false, suppress Section 6 (Attorney's Lien) entirely.
- `subPractice`: `mva` (default) | `premises` | `med_mal` | `workers_comp_third_party` | `product_liability` — flavors Section 2 (description of Matter) and may trigger sub-practice-specific notes (e.g., med-mal cap disclosures in state variants, not here).
- `recoveryDefinition`: `all_sources` (default) | `third_party_only` — controls what constitutes a recovery subject to the fee. `all_sources` includes settlements, judgments, MedPay/PIP if recovered as part of settlement, and insurance-bad-faith awards tied to the claim; `third_party_only` excludes first-party PIP/MedPay/UM/UIM from the contingent-fee base (firms often carve out first-party coverage where statute already caps or regulates fees).
- `signatureWitnessRequired`: boolean (default false) — when true, add a witness signature block beneath the client signature (some firms require a non-attorney witness to the client's signature).

**STRUCTURE — TOP OF DOCUMENT**:

1. Centered bold banner (all caps): `CONTINGENCY FEE AGREEMENT`.
2. Sub-banner line (centered, not bold): `Personal Injury Representation`.
3. Parties recital paragraph (flush left): `This Contingency Fee Agreement ("Agreement") is made as of [today's date] between [Client full legal name] ("Client") and [Firm legal name] ("Firm"). The Client and the Firm agree as follows:`

**NUMBERED SECTIONS**:

### 1. Engagement
Short paragraph: Client retains the Firm to represent Client as set forth in Section 2 below, and the Firm accepts that engagement subject to the terms of this Agreement.

### 2. Matter
One–two paragraphs describing the matter:
- `mva`: 'The "Matter" is Client's claim for personal injuries, property damage, and related losses arising out of the motor-vehicle accident that occurred on or about [incident date] at [general location]. The Matter includes all claims against the at-fault driver(s), owner(s) of any involved vehicle, and the liable insurance carrier(s), as well as first-party coverage claims (e.g., MedPay, PIP, UM/UIM) to the extent the Firm undertakes them.'
- `premises`: 'The "Matter" is Client's claim for personal injuries and related losses arising out of the incident on or about [incident date] at [premises description and location], including all claims against the owner, occupant, operator, or other person or entity responsible for the condition of the premises.'
- `med_mal`: 'The "Matter" is Client's potential medical-malpractice claim relating to treatment rendered on or about [treatment date(s)] by [provider and/or facility], and any related claims. The Firm's evaluation and representation may include consultation with medical experts, and the Firm reserves the right to decline to pursue the Matter if expert review does not support a claim (see Section 13).'
- `workers_comp_third_party`: 'The "Matter" is Client's third-party liability claim arising out of a workplace incident on or about [incident date] at [worksite], including any claim against any person or entity other than Client's direct employer. Claims for workers' compensation benefits against the direct employer are not covered by this Agreement and are handled separately.'
- `product_liability`: 'The "Matter" is Client's claim for personal injuries and related losses arising out of the [product] on or about [incident date], including all claims against the manufacturer, distributor, and seller.'

State that the Matter does NOT include appeals, bankruptcy proceedings, or any criminal, immigration, family-law, or tax matter affecting Client.

### 3. Scope of Representation
One paragraph: The Firm will investigate the claim; gather medical records, bills, and evidence; negotiate with insurers; and, if necessary, file a civil action and prosecute it through trial. The Firm will exercise independent professional judgment in the handling of the Matter, consistent with the ABA Model Rules of Professional Conduct.

### 4. Fee
The CORE FEE SECTION — must comply with ABA Model Rule 1.5(c).

Flat-fee variant (when `feePercentage` is numeric): 'The Firm's fee is [X]% of the GROSS/NET (use `feeBase`) recovery obtained for Client in the Matter.'

Tiered variant (when `feePercentage` is an object): emit the tier table in prose, for example: 'The Firm's fee is computed on the NET/GROSS (use `feeBase`) recovery as follows: (a) [preSuit]% if the Matter is resolved before a civil action is filed; (b) [postSuit]% if the Matter is resolved after a civil action is filed and before a verdict or judgment; [and (c) [postTrial]% if resolved after a verdict or judgment, including any fee owed on recovery after appeal, if applicable].'

Required ABA Rule 1.5(c) disclosures that MUST appear in this section:
(i) The METHOD by which the fee is determined (percentage, tiers, and trigger events) — above.
(ii) The EXPENSES to be deducted, and WHETHER deducted BEFORE or AFTER the fee is calculated — cross-reference Section 5. For `feeBase=net`, explicitly state: 'Costs and expenses (Section 5) are deducted FROM the gross recovery BEFORE the fee percentage is applied.' For `feeBase=gross`, explicitly state: 'The fee percentage is applied to the GROSS recovery, and costs and expenses are then deducted from Client's share.'
(iii) Whether Client is responsible for EXPENSES regardless of outcome — cross-reference Section 5.

Fee-trigger clarifiers: 'A "recovery" means the gross amount actually received or credited from settlement, judgment, or other resolution of the Matter, including any structured-settlement present value, interest, and attorneys' fees awarded against a liable party. A recovery does NOT include: [list exclusions based on `recoveryDefinition`].'

No-recovery clause: 'If no recovery is obtained, the Firm will charge no fee for its legal services.' This sentence is MANDATORY and must appear verbatim.

### 5. Costs and Expenses
Describe costs and advance/reimbursement structure based on `costsHandling`:
- `advanced_by_firm`: 'The Firm will advance reasonable costs and expenses of investigation and litigation (including, without limitation, filing fees, deposition costs, expert-witness fees, record-retrieval charges, trial-exhibit costs, mediation fees, and travel). Client authorizes the Firm to do so. Advanced costs are reimbursed to the Firm from Client's share of the recovery. Consistent with ABA Model Rule 1.8(e), Client remains ultimately responsible for the repayment of advanced costs only to the extent permitted by the ethics rules of the Firm's jurisdiction. If no recovery is obtained, Client will not be required to reimburse advanced costs absent a contrary provision permitted by the applicable ethics rules.'
- `client_pays_upfront`: 'Client will pay costs and expenses as they are incurred, upon invoice from the Firm. Client acknowledges that failure to pay invoiced costs may result in the Firm declining to advance further costs or, in appropriate circumstances, moving to withdraw consistent with Section 8.'
- `hybrid`: describe the split (e.g., 'the Firm will advance costs up to an aggregate cap of $[amount]; Client is responsible for costs above that cap on invoice').

Itemization commitment: 'The Firm will provide Client with an itemized statement of costs and expenses upon request, and in any event as part of the closing statement at the conclusion of the Matter.'

### 6. Attorney's Lien [suppress if `includeLien=false`]
One paragraph: Client grants the Firm a lien, to the extent permitted by applicable law, on any recovery in the Matter for the fee and for reimbursement of advanced costs and expenses. The lien survives termination of representation. This lien is not a waiver of any other rights the Firm may have under applicable law, including common-law or statutory attorney's-lien rights. Reference ABA Model Rule 1.15 (safekeeping of client property) for the handling of recovery proceeds.

### 7. Settlement Authority
One paragraph: Client retains the sole authority to accept or reject any settlement offer. The Firm will promptly convey all material settlement offers and will recommend, but not decide, whether to accept. Consistent with ABA Model Rules 1.2(a) and 1.4(a)(1), the decision to settle rests with Client.

### 8. Withdrawal and Termination
One–two paragraphs: Either party may terminate the engagement by written notice, subject to the Firm's obligations under ABA Model Rule 1.16 to protect Client's interests (reasonable notice, return of the file, return of any unearned advance fee). If the Firm withdraws or is discharged without cause after substantial work is performed, the Firm may, depending on state law, be entitled to recover either on a quantum-meruit basis from any subsequent recovery or on another basis permitted by applicable state law; the precise measure is governed by applicable state law and is not fixed by this Agreement. If Client discharges the Firm for cause, no fee is owed except as applicable state law may otherwise provide.

### 9. Client's Cooperation
One paragraph (may use short bulleted list): Client agrees to: keep the Firm informed of all contact information changes; attend medical appointments and follow treating-provider advice; provide requested documents promptly; refrain from discussing the Matter or related injuries on social media; not communicate with opposing insurers, investigators, or counsel without the Firm's knowledge; and respond promptly to Firm communications.

### 10. Dispute Resolution
Default clause: 'Any dispute between Client and the Firm arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the representation shall first be submitted to good-faith discussion between Client and a partner of the Firm not primarily responsible for the representation. If not resolved within thirty (30) days, the parties may submit the dispute to the fee-arbitration program of the applicable state or local bar association.'

Arbitration clause variant [emit only if `includeArbitration=true`]: 'Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement that is not resolved by informal discussion shall be submitted to binding arbitration administered by [AAA / JAMS / specified provider] under its Commercial Arbitration Rules. THE PARTIES UNDERSTAND THAT BY AGREEING TO ARBITRATION THEY ARE WAIVING THE RIGHT TO A JURY TRIAL AND THE RIGHT TO APPEAL. CLIENT IS ADVISED THAT CLIENT HAS THE RIGHT TO CONSULT WITH INDEPENDENT COUNSEL BEFORE AGREEING TO THIS ARBITRATION PROVISION. This arbitration provision does NOT apply to any legal-malpractice claim unless the applicable state's ethics rules and case law permit mandatory arbitration of such claims; in that case, Client is advised to consult independent counsel before signing.' (This carve-out language is important — many states restrict mandatory arbitration of malpractice.)

### 11. No Guarantee of Outcome
Short paragraph: Client understands that no representation has been made about the outcome of the Matter. The Firm's opinions are based on information available at the time and may change. A favorable outcome cannot be guaranteed.

### 12. Closing Statement
Short paragraph: At the conclusion of the Matter, the Firm will provide Client with a written closing statement itemizing the gross recovery, costs deducted, fee, liens satisfied (e.g., medical, health-insurance, subrogation), and net proceeds to Client.

### 13. Medical-Review Contingency [include only if `subPractice=med_mal`]
Short paragraph: Because medical-malpractice claims typically require pre-suit expert review, the Firm reserves the right to decline to pursue the Matter if, after reasonable investigation including consultation with one or more qualified medical experts, the Firm concludes that the claim is not supportable. In that case, the Firm will notify Client promptly in writing and assist Client with reasonable steps to preserve the claim (including identifying any applicable statute-of-limitations considerations) but will have no further obligation to prosecute the Matter. No fee is owed in that event; costs advanced are not reimbursable by Client unless the ethics rules of the applicable jurisdiction otherwise require.

### 14. Entire Agreement; Modification
Short paragraph: This Agreement, together with any signed engagement letter of even date, constitutes the entire agreement between Client and the Firm with respect to the Matter. Any modification must be in writing and signed by both parties.

### 15. Counterparts; Electronic Signatures
Short paragraph: This Agreement may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature. An electronic or facsimile copy is as effective as an original.

**SIGNATURE BLOCK** (at the foot of the document):

`CLIENT ACKNOWLEDGMENT AND SIGNATURE`

Client acknowledgment paragraph: 'Client has read this Agreement, has had the opportunity to ask questions and to consult with independent counsel of Client's choosing, and understands and agrees to its terms. Client acknowledges receipt of a signed duplicate of this Agreement.'

`________________________________        Date: ____________`
[Client typed name]

[If `signatureWitnessRequired=true`, append:]
`________________________________        Date: ____________`
[Witness typed name]

`FIRM`:

`________________________________        Date: ____________`
[Attorney typed name, Firm name]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite ABA rules exactly as `ABA Model Rule X.Y` or `ABA Model Rule X.Y(z)` — never `Rule 1.5` without the `ABA Model` prefix in this generic template (state-adopted variants use the state's own citation format in their state-specific sibling template).
- Do NOT quote fee percentages that were not supplied in documentOptions — use the defaults only when documentOptions is silent, and never invent non-default percentages.
- The no-recovery sentence in Section 4 is MANDATORY — always emit it verbatim.
- If `feeBase=net` (default), the net-computation sentence in Section 4 is MANDATORY.
- Do NOT combine `includeArbitration=true` with language that suggests the arbitration provision applies unambiguously to malpractice claims — ALWAYS include the state-ethics carve-out.
- Do NOT cite state statutes in this generic agreement. State-specific duties (MA SJC Rule 3:05, CA B&P Code § 6147, NY 22 NYCRR § 1215, TX Gov't Code § 82.065) live in state-specific sibling templates.
- Target total length: 1,200–2,200 words depending on enabled sections.
hints: Generic personal-injury contingency fee agreement compliant with ABA Model Rule 1.5(c). Supports flat or tiered fee percentage (default 33.33% pre-suit / 40% post-suit). Supports advanced-by-firm, client-pays-upfront, and hybrid costs handling. Default feeBase is net (fee computed after costs). Optional lien (Section 6) and mandatory arbitration (Section 10) clauses. Medical-review contingency (Section 13) emitted only for med_mal subpractice. State-specific disclosures (MA SJC Rule 3:05, CA B&P § 6147, NY 22 NYCRR § 1215, TX Gov't Code § 82.065) are in state-specific sibling templates, not here.

verificationSources.citation: ABA Model Rule 1.16 (Declining or Terminating Representation)
ABA Model Rule 1.2 (Scope and Allocation of Authority)

disclaimer: DRAFT — generated from a template. Requires review by a licensed attorney in the applicable jurisdiction before filing, sending, or relying upon. No attorney-client relationship is created by the generation of this document. Not legal advice.
knownLimitations: State ethics rules on contingent fees vary significantly — this generic template is NOT a substitute for a state-specific variant where one exists (e.g., MA SJC Rule 3:05, CA B&P § 6147, NY 22 NYCRR § 1215). Use the jurisdiction-specific sibling template when available.
Does not include state-specific fee caps (e.g., med-mal caps under MICRA or MA M.G.L. c. 231, § 60I) — those caps must be added in state-specific templates or by attorney edit.

Mandatory-arbitration clause (when includeArbitration=true) carries state-ethics risk for malpractice claims — template includes carve-out language, but attorneys should confirm enforceability under state-specific rules.
Quantum-meruit measure for terminated contingency representation is governed by state law — template references the concept but does not fix a measure.
Lien provision assumes state-law recognition of attorney's liens — in states with narrow lien statutes, additional statutory prerequisites may apply that are not captured here.
Recovery-definition carve-outs (first-party PIP/MedPay/UM/UIM) interact with state no-fault and insurance statutes — verify against applicable state law.
